text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, there lived a cat named Whiskers. Whiskers had a favorite spot on the window ledge near the kitchen window. Every day after breakfast, she'd stretch out there feeling the cool air.

One evening, as the sun dipped below the forest line, casting a golden hue through the window, Whiskers noticed something strange. On the kitchen counter lay a plate piled high with cookies. She'd never been so brazen. She nibbled a few while watching a squirrel dancing merrily outside.

The following morning, curious as ever, she nibbled her way through a dozen cookies. Her little orange paws were covered in crumbs.

Just then, Mrs. Flufflebum noticed Whiskers munching away. "Goodness, Whiskers! You're eating like a little fox! That cookie won't last forever." She chuckled.

Whiskers looked up at her with big, innocent eyes. "Oh, Mrs. Flufflebum,"
